vael_intro.title: Air
vael_intro.narrator: Vael. Air carrier. What he avoids saying.
vael_intro.text:
The problem with air,
It's that we only notice when he's missing.

Or when he pushes someone into a fountain.

I will immediately state:
The fountain was already there.

I, technically,
I only presented two elements.

Water.

And the merchant shouting too loud.

vael_pourquoi_air.title: Why Air
vael_pourquoi_air.text:
The air has nothing.

He's passing.
He's touching.
He's leaving again.

It's convenient.

When you stay too long somewhere,
People are starting to ask questions.

**"Where did you come from? *
**"Why are you smiling like that? *
**"Is it you who hung the underwear of the provost at the bell tower? *

Yes.

But that's not the point.

The subject is that air can enter everywhere
without asking permission.

And get out before we ask him to account.

vael_fuite.title: A Very Elegant Flight
vael_fuite.text:
Run is a hard word.

I prefer:
Strategic repositioning with plume.

But yes.

Maybe.

When I was a child,
I already knew how to disappear from a room
Before someone gets angry.

What was useful,
since I was often the reason
of this anger.

My father said:

**"Vael, one day your humor will kill you. *

I said:

**"So I hope he'll do it quickly,
Because I hate long speeches. *

He didn't laugh.

My mother does.

Discreetly.

It's important, discreet victories.

vael_enfance.title: The Roofs
vael_enfance.text:
I was born in a city where the streets went up more than they went along.

The houses were tight.
The balconies too close.
The roofs almost polished by the children running on it.

Finally.

By me.

The other children had more respect for gravity.

Character weakness.

My mother was a messenger.
My father repaired the girouettes, the bells and the things placed far too high
For reasonable people.

He said:

**"If you fall, scream before touching the ground. *

Strange advice.

Very useful later.

vael_mere.title: The Messenger
vael_mere.text:
My mother knew the whole town.

Not the streets.

Shortcuts.

The difference is huge.

A street leads you where everyone goes.
A shortcut leads you where no one's waiting for you.

She taught me how to listen to the doors,
the steps,
poorly closed windows,
silences that last one second too long.

**"People talk even when they keep quiet.** she said.

I was listening.

Then I repeated at the wrong time.

That's how we learn diplomacy.

Or being chased by a baker.

Both look more like each other than we think.

vael_premiere_chute.title: The First Fall
vael_premiere_chute.location: Upper Town — Roof of the old mill
vael_premiere_chute.text:
I was seven.

I was running on the roof of the old mill.

Forbidden.
Dangerous.
Very fun.

It was raining a little.

Not enough to stop.
Just enough to make stupid a child already very committed in this direction.

My foot slipped.

I turned.

The world has turned.
The sky is under me.
The city above.

I screamed.

Not out of fear.

Because my father told me to.

And the air caught me.

vael_air_rattrape.title: Trapped
vael_air_rattrape.text:
Not like arms.

Rather like a very firm idea
deciding the ground could wait.

I stayed suspended.

Head down.
Open arms.
A missing shoe.

An old woman looked at me from her window.

**"You again? *

I said,

*"Technically, I'm not really there anymore. *

She closed her shutters.

The air gently put me in a laundry cart.

White.
Clean.

Not for long.

vael_peur_chute.title: Fear?
vael_peur_chute.location: Upper Town — Between sky and ground
vael_peur_chute.text:
Yes.

Of course.

I'm funny,
Not unconscious.

Finally.

Not completely.

Fear first came into the belly.
Then in the throat.
Then in your hands.

And just before the ground,
She's become something else.

An opening.

As if the world were saying:

You can fall. *

Or you can go somewhere else. *

I chose somewhere else.

I often choose somewhere else.

vael_reaction_parents.title: Parents
vael_reaction_parents.text:
My mom laughed.

Not yet.

First she checked my arms,
My legs,
My head.

Especially my head.

**"She was like that before. "** said my father.

Then she laughed.

My father didn't laugh.

He looked at the window open.
The curtains that moved without wind.
I'm covered in wet linen.

**"You called. *

**"I shouted. *

**"And something answered. *

It was the first time I saw my father seriously.
Without being angry.

I didn't like it.

So I made a joke.

Bad idea.

As often.

vael_mauvaise_blague.title: Bad idea
vael_mauvaise_blague.text:
I said,

**"At least now we know that I fall with talent. *

Quiet.

Long.

My father closed his eyes.

My mother looked at the ceiling.

I think she was praying.

Not for my survival.

For his patience.

Then my father said:

**"You must not show this. *

And there,
The room has become smaller.

The windows, too.

Even the air seemed reluctant to come in.

vael_apprendre.title: Learn to Pass
vael_apprendre.text:
I learned alone.

I mean, alone with the air.

Which is a phrase that normal people avoid saying.

In the morning I threw feathers from the roofs.
I was trying to spin them.
Then go up.
Then enter through an open window.

One day I did it.

In soup.

To the provost.

I'm not gonna pretend it wasn't voluntary.
That would be unworthy of both of us.

I heard the air doesn't force.

He's bypassing.
He raises.
He's hijacking.
It just grows enough for things to choose almost by themselves.

vael_limites.title: Limits
vael_limites.text:
The air limit,
It's that he doesn't last long.

A stone,
We can put it down.

A flame,
We can feed her.

Water,
We can collect it.

The air?

The air is escaping.

Me too.

I could slow down a fall.
Move an arrow.
Wear a whisper from room to room.

But if I tried to keep something too long,
My hands were shaking.
My breath was cutting.

As if the world reminded me:

*What is passing is not done to stay. *

Beautiful phrase.

Very annoying when you want to keep someone.

vael_recrutement.title: The Man Who Was Not There
vael_recrutement.text:
He came in one night.

Finally.

Arrived.

I noticed it mostly because he was already sitting next to me.
on the roof of the bell tower.

Which is rude.

The roofs have a label.
We don't come up on people's roofs.

He was wearing white and grey.
His hair moved when there was no wind.

Frozen.

**"Did you feel me coming? *

**"No. *

**"Good. *

**"I find it questionable. *

He smiled.

*"You are Vael. *

**"It depends who asks. *

vael_esprit_air.title: The Spirit of the Air
vael_esprit_air.text:
His name was Eolan.

Or he said his name was Eolan.

With the air,
You always have to keep a small margin of suspicion.

He explained the Order to me.
The Five Blades.
The Chaos.
Prophecy.
Spirits.

A lot of information for a roof.

I asked:

**"What if I refuse? *

He looked at the city.

**"Then you stay. *

**"That's it? *

**"Yes. *

I was worried.

People who really let you choose
are much more dangerous than those who command.

vael_depart.title: Departure
vael_depart.text:
My parents listened.

My father standing.
My mother is sitting.
Me on the edge of the window,
Which didn't help the mood.

**"Go down there. "** said my father.

**« Symbolically or physically? *

**"Vael. *

I went down.

Eolan spoke softly.

At the end,
My mother gave me her old messenger bag.

**« Shortcuts are useless
If you forget where you wanted to go. *

My father gave me a broken little guru.

**"To find out where the wind comes from. *

I said,

**"Am I supposed to be touched or insulted? *

He squeezed me against him.

Touched, then.

vael_aurenthal.title: Auenthal
vael_aurenthal.text:
Aurenthal was huge.

Which is a polite way of saying:
far too sure of her.

The Temple, too.

High columns.
Solemn silence.
Statues looking at you like they paid the entrance.

Three children were waiting.

A girl with silver hair.
Very quiet.
Too quiet.
The very quiet people always hide either great wisdom,
Or a desire to hit someone with a chair.

A boy who looked like a standing argument.

And a girl smiling like she decided everyone deserved a chance.

I thought,

She's brave. *

Or misinformed.

vael_ikar_debut.title: Ikar — Bad weather
vael_ikar_debut.text:
Ikar didn't greet me.

He looked at me.

As you look at a door squeaking.

I said,

**"Enchanted too. *

He didn't answer.

The third day,
I made her blade disappear during training.

Little missing.
Very temporary.
Almost educational.

He burned the floor.

**"Very funny. *

**"I wanted to see if you were panicking. *

**"And? *

**"You turned the yard into toast. *
**"So yes, but with style. *

He almost smiled.

Failing.

I considered it a military victory.

vael_aryn_debut.title: Aryn — Too Calm
vael_aryn_debut.text:
Aryn didn't talk much.

Which immediately seemed suspicious.

Not because she was hiding something.

Because she was really listening.

People who really listen are terrible.
They hear things we didn't say.

One night she asked me:

**"You always joke when you're scared? *

I said,

**"No. Sometimes I joke when others are afraid. *

She looked at me.

Long time.

**"So yes. *

I hated it.

I respected her a lot later.

vael_entrainement.title: Training
vael_entrainement.text:
Eolan rarely trained me on the ground.

**"The soil gives bad habits. *

Typical phrase of someone who never had to clean his shoes.

He made me run on the roofs of the Temple.
Jump between towers.
Listen to conversations through three walls.
Catch a leaf before it touches the water.

Then he disappeared.

In the middle of a sentence.

The first time I yelled:

**"It's very rude! *

His voice answered behind me:

**"It's very effective. *

I liked him.

It was unbearable.

choices.text: He was talking to you about the others?

vael_eolan_autres.title: Eolan and Others
vael_eolan_autres.text:
Eolan said the others were easy to read.

Which was wrong.

But he said many things wrong
to see if I was correcting them.

**"The fire is moving forward. *

**"To the wall. *

**"Water bypasses. *

**"And ends up in the shoes. *

**"The earth remains. *

I didn't answer that.

He smiled.

**"What about the air? *

I raised my shoulders.

**"The air pretends to leave. *

Eolan looked far ahead.

**"Yes. *

It was the first time he looked sad.

Very briefly.

Like a stream of air that we think we invented.

vael_pret_a_tout.title: Ready for All
vael_pret_a_tout.location: Temple of the Order — East Tower
vael_pret_a_tout.text:
I was ten when Eolan said the sentence.

The big one.
The famous one.
Very practical when trying to traumatize children with elegance.

**"The Chaos bearer will come. *

**"He could warn before? *

**"Vael. *

**"I'm sorry. Keep going. I interrupt the prophecy. *

He didn't laugh.

Bad sign.

**"You will have to be ready for everything. *

I looked at the void under the tower.

The wind was rising.

**"Everything is a very broad word. *

**"Yes. *

**"Practical to explain nothing. *

He didn't answer.

So I realized I was right.

vael_compris_tout.title: What I understood
vael_compris_tout.location: Temple of the Order — East Tower
vael_compris_tout.text:
I realized nobody really knew.

Not completely.

The spirits had seen Caël.
They had seen what fear had done to him.

And instead of giving us an answer,
They gave us a sentence.

* Ready for anything. *

Ikar heard:
If necessary. *

Taera heard:
Never give up. *

Aryn heard:
* Look again. *

Me?

I heard the silence about the word "all."

What is not said is sometimes louder
What we shout.

vael_blague_tout.title: The Blague
vael_blague_tout.location: Temple of the Order — East Tower
vael_blague_tout.text:
I said,

**« Ready for anything, even boiled vegetables from the refectory? *

Eolan looked at me.

**"Prior to this. *

I had a laugh.

A real one.

Then he added:

**"One day you will joke in front of someone who believes you are making fun of his pain. *

I stopped.

**"I don't do that. *

**"Not voluntarily. *

The wind has become cold.

I wasn't hungry anymore.

Not even to complain about vegetables.

That's to say.

vael_annees.title: Years
vael_annees.location: Temple of the Order — Everywhere except where I was wanted
vael_annees.text:
We were growing up.

Aryn was getting quieter,
which seemed impossible and yet.

Ikar was getting better with a blade,
and worse with conversations.

Taera grew things in every corner available.
One day she planted mint near my window.

**"To make it smell good. *

**"I felt bad? *

**"No. *

Break.

**"Your room, maybe. *

Trahison.

I traveled a lot in the Temple.
The corridors.
The roofs.
Archives.
Prohibited places.

Especially the forbidden places.

They're always better ventilated.

vael_archives.title: The Archives
vael_archives.location: Temple of the Order — Prohibited Archives
vael_archives.text:
I wasn't entering the forbidden archives.

It's important to make that clear.

I was passing through.

The difference is legal.

One night I found Aryn there.
She was reading an old story about Cael.

**"You shouldn't be here.** she said.

**"Neither do you. *

**"I'm looking for something. *

*"Me too. *

**"What? *

I looked at the shelves.

**"A way out, now you've seen me. *

She almost smiled.

Then she showed me the text.

The ancient Taera had reached out to Caël.

And for a second,
It worked.

vael_quinze_ans.title: Fifteen Years
vael_quinze_ans.text:
At 15,
I was officially unbearable.

Officially too.

But I was helpful.

I could hear a conversation at the other end of a court.
I knew how to drop a sword with one hand without touching the arm.
I knew how to disappear in a straight corridor,
which is a great agitation to methodical people.

Ikar, then.

But above all,
I knew when someone was laughing.

That's very specific.

Aryn rarely lied.
You'll never be very good.
Ikar was lying like a burning door.

Me?

I was excellent.

That's why I used to be quick.

vael_mensonge.title: The Lie
vael_mensonge.location: Temple of the Order — North Roof
vael_mensonge.text:
I was joking when I was scared.

I was joking when others suffered,
Not to deny their pain,
but to leave them a walk where to set foot.

A little walk.

Sometimes it worked.

Sometimes not.

The problem,
It's because I started using this walk for myself too.

By force,
I didn't know if I made everyone laugh.
Or if I covered the sound of something falling within me.

I could have talked about it.

To Aryn.
To Taera.
Even in Ikar, if I had planned a armor.

I didn't.

I made a joke.

Classic.

vael_nuit_signal.title: The Signal
vael_nuit_signal.location: Temple of the Order — Temple Roof
vael_nuit_signal.text:
The night the Chaos woke up,
The wind stopped.

Completely.

You know what's worse than a storm?

The lack of wind.

Because here,
Even the air holds its breath.

I was on the roof of the Temple.
Of course.

My blade disappeared from its sheath.

Then she came back.

Then she disappeared again.

**"Very mature. "** I said.

She came back into my hand.

Cold.
Light.
Worried.

I didn't know a sword could be worried.

Mine always had a lot of personality.

vael_sentir_chaos.title: What I have Senti
vael_sentir_chaos.text:
Not anger.

Not just.

The Chaos made noise,
But in the middle there was a vacuum.

Like someone calling
I don't know what name to say.

I thought of Caël.

To the prophecy.
To all the people who had been afraid of him
before you even know him.

Then I thought,

He's alone. *

And immediately after:

I know that. *

The wind is back.

Just enough to push me to the stairs.

Very subtle.

For an invisible kick.

vael_reveiller_autres.title: Awakening Others
vael_reveiller_autres.text:
I went to wake the others.

Finally.

Try.

Aryn was already up.
Taera, too.
Ikar was already wearing his armor.

Very vexing.

It looked like I was late for my own alert.

**"I had prepared a dramatic entry. *

**"Not now.** said Ikar.

**"It's always for a dramatic entry. *

Aryn looked at me.

That look meant:
*Vael. *

Not my first name.

The whole concept.

I raised my hands.

**"Okay. Bad time. *

Then I added:

**"But excellent narrative tension. *

Taera smiled.

A little.

That was enough.

vael_ikar_nuit.title: Ikar that night
vael_ikar_nuit.text:
Ikar had this face.

The people who have already decided
And who hope someone stops them
without forcing them to ask.

I saw him right away.

I pretended I didn't see anything.

That's my talent.

But in the courtyard, before we leave,
I caught him.

**"Ikar. *

**"What. *

**"You don't have to wear this alone. *

He didn't say anything.

**"There are four of us. "** I added.
**"Five soon. *
**"The weight is divided. *

He looked at me.

Not the look he usually looks at me.
Not "what else you want."

Something else.

He shook his head.

Then he went back to his horse.

He slowed down a little.

Just a little.

vael_depart_temple.title: Departure
vael_depart_temple.text:
Four silhouettes in the courtyard.

Aryn calm.
Taera solid.
Ikar on fire, even when he wasn't burning.

Me,
Trying not to whistle.

I held thirty seconds.

Eolan appeared one last time.

He wasn't here.
It's important to make that clear.

But he was not there in a very present way.

He looked at me.

**"You know what to do. *

**"I especially know what should not be done. *

**"It's often the same. *

He's gone.

I whistled a little.

Not on purpose.

Ikar sighed.

Travel well gone.

choices.text: Look at the Temple one last time.

vael_route.title: The Road
vael_route.text:
The road was long.

So I whistled.

The road was disturbing.

So I whistled better.

Ikar hated it.
Which proved that I still had a positive influence on the world.

Half way,
I caught him.

**"You don't have to wear this alone. *

He said he was wearing nothing.

Of course.

Aryn wasn't talking.
Taera sometimes touched the ground from his horse.

I was listening to the wind.

He was from the east.
From Cendral.

He smelled ash.

And something else.

Someone who had just lost their whole world
And still breathing.

vael_pensee_joueur.title: The Player
vael_pensee_joueur.text:
I didn't know him yet.

The Chaos bearer.

The boy of prophecy.
The traveling disaster.
The future end of the world according to people who were probably selling anti-happiness amulets in front of the Temple.

I didn't know him.

But I already knew one thing.

Everyone would look at it as an answer.

One yes.
A no.
A threat.
A miracle.
A gun.
A mistake.

Nobody likes being reduced to a word.

Me,
I was often reduced to a joke.

It's lighter.

But it still weighs.

vael_fumee.title: Smoke
vael_fumee.text:
We saw the smoke.

Black.
Thick.
Very dramatic.

Honestly,
If the prophecy had hired a decorator,
She wouldn't have done better.

I didn't say that.

Because, nineteen-year-old Vael,
sometimes bears traces of accidental wisdom.

My blade is gone.

Then she came back into my hand.

Aryn closed his eyes.

**"He's still here. *

Taera laid her hand on the floor.

**"He is alive. *

Ikar looked at the smoke.
Long time.

He said nothing.

So for once,
I didn't say anything either.

The wind came from Cendral.

He was shaking.

choices.text: Go back to the main story.

vael_derniere_pensee.title: The Last Thought
vael_derniere_pensee.text:
My mother said shortcuts are useless.
If we forget where we wanted to go.

My father said you had to scream before you hit the ground.

Eolan said a lot of things,
then disappeared before he could be asked for clarification.

Very practical.
Very cowardly.
Very airy.

In front of the smoke,
I understood a simple thing.

The boy over there was falling.

Everybody was gonna yell at him what to do.

Stop.
Control.
Come back.
Don't destroy.
Don't be that.
Don't become him.

Maybe he needed it.
to hear someone yelling with him.

Right before the ground.

choices.text: Go back to the main story.

fin_memoire_vael.title: End — Memory of Vael
fin_memoire_vael.text:
Memory passes.

Like air — no noise, no visible trace.

choices.text: Go back to the main story.

vael_fuite_detail.title: What it really was
vael_fuite_detail.text:
It was several things.

First the questions.

Village people asked a lot of questions.
What are you gonna do next? *
Do you look like your father or your mother? *
Are you going to stay here all your life? *

I didn't have the answers.

So I was leaving.

On the roofs.
Nobody was asking questions up there.

Neither did the air demand anything.

He just agreed to be here.

That was enough.

vael_mere_ressemble.title: Resemblance
vael_mere_ressemble.text:
Everyone says so.

Same way to look aside.
Same way to answer next door.
Same way to disappear before we find out.

She thought it was funny.

**"You'll be a messenger too? *

**"I will deliver the air. *

**"The air is not flowing. *

**" Exactly. *

She laughed.

My mother laughed with all her body.

People who laugh with all their bodies
Are not afraid of anything.

Or they learned to pretend
Better than everyone else.

I never knew which one.

vael_eolan_phrase.title: The Great Phrase
vael_eolan_phrase.text:
Eolan said things like:

**"The air is everywhere and nowhere. *

**"Very useful. I was answering.

**"The air sees everything without being seen. *

**"Even more useful. *

**"Air..."**

**"Eolan. *

**"What. *

**"Just say what you mean. *

He'd been quiet.

Then:

**"The air understands people better than people understand each other. *
**"Because he hears them think. *

**"Here. "** I said.
**"It wasn't that hard. *

He was missing.

I think I had annoyed him.

We were getting along.

vael_taera_debut.title: Taera — The Garden
vael_taera_debut.text:
Taera had a garden.

In the inner courtyard.

I didn't understand why.

**"What's the use? *

**"It grows. *

**"So what? *

She looked at me.

**"Have you ever watched anything grow? *

**"No. *

**"Sit down. *

I sat down.

We looked.

Twenty minutes.

Nothing.

**"It doesn't grow fast. *

**"No. *

**"Is that the point? *

**"This is one of the interests. *

I didn't understand.

I came back the next day anyway.

And the next day.

There was nothing to do.
Just be there.

It was relaxing.

vael_dernier_regard.title: The Last Look
vael_dernier_regard.text:
I didn't plan to do it.

But when I came out, I looked up.

The high columns.
Solemn silence.

Twelve years.

No need to count memories —
They come alone when you look long enough.

The first day in the dining room.
Ikar crossed his arms.
Aryn was looking at his hands.
Taera smiling at everyone.

I'm the one who made my own plate disappear by accident.

We all seemed surprised.

That was the beginning of something.

I looked down.

**"Come?** said Aryn.

**"I was looking at the clouds. *

She knew I was lying.

She didn't say anything.

That's why we get along.

vael_reconnait_vide.title: The Recognised Empty
vael_reconnait_vide.text:
Yes.

Because I had the same one.

Not the same size.
Not the same kind.

But this moment when you're looking
something to hang on to
And find nothing.

Prophecy.
The Chaos.
Destiny.

Big words.

But underneath there was just someone
who wanted to help
And that broke everything.

I knew what it was.

Not the Chaos.

Shame.

And shame without anyone to say
It's not your fault.

It's becoming something else.

Something much more dangerous.

vael_cri.title: The Cree
vael_cri.text:
I hadn't yelled yet.

Not found what yet.

My father cried out. *

It was practical but not very inspired.

My mother cried out very loud
And the vase had changed direction.

I was still looking.

Something useful.
Something real.
Something that looks like what I really wanted to say.

We'd see.

The smoke was there.

I hadn't yelled yet.

But for the first time in a long time —

I really had something to say.

choices.text: Go back to the main story.
